pattern: Each candidate word is independently tested against the front of the target string, so a single linear pass with a built-in prefix test tallies the matches.
hints: You only care whether each word lines up with the very start of the target, not where else it might appear.
Most languages can answer "does s begin with this word?" directly; count how many words pass that test.
Iterate over words and increment a counter whenever s starts with that word, including repeated identical words which each count separately.

edge_inputs.description: word longer than s can never be a prefix
matching first letter but diverging later

stress.description: many short random words against a short random target
stress.note: biased so roughly half the words are genuine prefixes
